channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: European troops, US support are part of an emerging plan for Ukraine. But it faces many hurdles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As the U.S. and Russia push ahead – so far without Ukraine at the table – on talks to end the war, political and military leaders in Europe are fleshing out details of a plan for European forces to help ensure Moscow does not attack again.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians rally around their president after Trump seeks to denigrate him
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Days before the third anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukrainians are as somber and tense as they were right before Moscow launched the war. Only now, they aren’t just worried about their longtime enemy.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia's top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, MICHELLE GUMEDE and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow homes and history
item.description: CHINGUETTI, Mauritania (AP) — For centuries, poets, scholars and theologians have flocked to Chinguetti, a trans-Saharan trading post home to more than a dozen libraries containing thousands of manuscripts. But it now stands on the brink of oblivion.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bodies released by Hamas include some of the oldest and youngest hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The bodies of some of the oldest and youngest hostages taken to Gaza at the start of the Israel-Hamas war were released Thursday, bringing the total closer to the 33 supposed to be freed under the ceasefire that started last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel grieves as Hamas turns over the remains of the young Bibas brothers
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Ariel and Kfir Bibas — two of the youngest hostages held in Gaza — became a symbol for Israelis of the brutality of Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facing pressure from Trump, Costa Rica and Honduras join Panama as stopovers for foreign deportees
item.description: SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica (AP) — A group of families and children hailing from Uzbekistan, China, Afghanistan, Russia and more countries climbed down the stairs of an airplane in Costa Rica's capital Thursday, the first flight of deportees from other nations Costa Rica agreed to hold in detention facilit
item.dc:creator: JAVIER CÓRDOBA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge rules Trump admin violated order to unfreeze foreign aid but declines to hold officials in contempt
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration violated a court order to temporarily unfreeze foreign aid but declined to hold officials in civil contempt over the transgression. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Israel's UN ambassador slams Hamas' 'evil and depraved' display of hostages' coffins
item.description: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk condemned Hamas' parading of the coffins containing Shiri, Ariel and Kfir Bibas and Oded Lifshitz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Guterres to propose UN assumes funding for mission struggling to fight gangs in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres said he would request that the United Nations assumes funding for the structural and logistical expenses of a multinational force in Haiti that is struggling to fight violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans in new survey say Russia started war in Ukraine
item.description: Most Americans think Russia started the war in Ukraine, with those who said Kyiv was first to attack were in single digits, according to a survey published Wednesday. The YouGov poll found that 61 percent of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainians shocked, defiant in face of Trump attacks on Zelensky
item.description: Ukrainian officials feel betrayed but defiant after President Trump this week blamed their president for Russia’s invasion, as the U.S. pursues talks with Vladimir Putin to end the war Russia’s president started three years ago. Desperately reliant on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Philippine village offers cash for mosquitos amid dengue outbreak
item.description: To combat a dengue outbreak, a Philippine village is offering a bounty of one Philippines peso for every five mosquitoes residents turn in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says Europe 'on the cusp of peace' as Trump pushes for talks with Russia
item.description: NATIONAL HARBOR, Md. — Vice President Vance defended President Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine War on Thursday as the president ramps up his criticism of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: Lula says Bolsonaro’s push for amnesty means he's admitting guilt in Brazil coup plot
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said Thursday that his predecessor, Jair Bolsonaro, is acknowledging his guilt in a case in which he's accused of plotting a coup to stay in power after his 2022 electoral defeat by focusing on a campaign for amnesty.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis got out of bed to eat breakfast on 7th day in hospital: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis, who has been hospitalized since Feb. 14, apparently got out of bed for breakfast and is showing "slight improvement" in his medical tests, according to Vatican News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia seems to have time on its side as the war in Ukraine reaches its third year
item.description: When Russian troops rolled into Ukraine three years ago, they brought their parade uniforms with them on the push to Kyiv. President Vladimir Putin expected a quick victory. What Putin dubbed the “special military operation” has turned into Europe’s largest conflict since World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy says he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia’s top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome And Michelle Gumede, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 slain Israelis, including Shiri Bibas and her two young boys
item.description: Hamas has turned over the bodies of four slain Israeli hostages more than 500 days after the terrorist group's Oct. 7, 2023, attack in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: The Israeli military says the remains of two child hostages have been identified but another body released by Hamas was not the boys’ mother.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Jahjouh, Wafaa Shurafa And Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico president says her government requested US surveillance drone flights
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Wednesday that surveillance drone flights by the U.S. government over Mexico are occurring in collaboration with and at the request of her government. They come as U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look at the 8 Latin American crime groups designated as terrorist organizations by the US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The United States government is formally designating eight Latin American organized crime groups that also operate in the U.S. to be “foreign terrorist organizations.” They are involved in drug trafficking, migrant smuggling and extend their territory through violence.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement, receives visit from Italian PM Giorgia Meloni
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in the hospital on Wednesday where she spent 20 minutes with the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on remote Australian beach
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that became stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane over the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea; the plane did not have to alter its path to avoid a collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Now is an 'opportune' time to fix FAA: Former official Billy Nolen
item.description: "We certainly can never rest on our laurels when it comes to keeping the flying public safe," Nolen said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Gold mine collapse kills 42 in Mali
item.description: Late on Saturday, Malian television announced the collapse of the site at Bilali Koto in the commune of Dabia, with a provisional death toll of 42 and many injured.
item.dc:creator: BABA AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
